name: Precious Metals Refinery
description_short: Precious Metals Refinery buys gold, silver, platinum, diamonds, fine jewelry, luxury watches, and coins at 10+ retail locations across Arizona and California.
description_long:
Precious Metals Refinery (PMR) was founded in 2011 by Robert Chorost and David Schiesel and is headquartered in Tucson, Arizona. Operating as an LLC, the company has grown to over ten retail locations across Arizona (Tucson, Phoenix, Marana, Green Valley) and California (Palm Springs, Palm Desert, Carlsbad, La Jolla, Mission Valley, Newport Beach). Unlike traditional pawn shops, PMR positions itself as a specialist precious metals buyer with GIA-certified gemologists and Graduate Gemologists on staff — credentials issued by the Gemological Institute of America, the gold standard for jewelry valuation.

PMR's core business is buying precious metals and valuables directly from the public. They purchase gold in any form — jewelry, scrap, and bullion — along with silver (flatware, coins, bullion), platinum, loose diamonds and gemstones, fine and estate jewelry, luxury watches (Rolex, Cartier, Patek Philippe and others), graded coins (PCGS- and NGC-certified), and antiques. Every visit begins with a free in-person evaluation. The company also offers jewelry and watch repair services at select locations. There are no fixed pricing tiers; offers are determined item by item at the time of evaluation.

PMR's key differentiators center on in-house capability. They perform on-site metal testing using a Thermo Fisher Scientific XRF Analyzer, which they say eliminates guesswork and speeds up the evaluation. Because PMR conducts in-house refining rather than selling to an outside refiner, they claim to offer payouts up to 40% higher than typical gold buyers — though this figure is a marketing claim and has not been independently verified. Evaluations are conducted in private buying offices where items never leave the seller's sight, and staff includes coin numismatists and dedicated luxury watch experts in addition to gemologists.

PMR's 5.0 Google rating across 90 reviews reflects strong customer satisfaction, particularly among estate sellers and collectors who value the specialist staff. The main limitations to understand going in: once a seller accepts an offer, the transaction is final — there is no return or reconsideration process. The "up to 40% more" claim is unverified marketing. BBB data is conflicting across PMR's Arizona and California listings, and complaint records include at least one instance of a customer dispute involving alleged threats over a negative review. Sellers with high-value items should consider getting a competing offer before committing.

pricing.guarantee_details: No money-back guarantee. All purchases are final once the seller accepts an offer.

pros: GIA-certified gemologists and Graduate Gemologists on staff for accurate diamond and fine jewelry valuation
In-house refining claimed to enable payouts up to 40% above typical gold buyers by cutting out middlemen
Thermo Fisher Scientific XRF Analyzer used on-site for same-visit metal composition testing
5.0/5 Google rating from 90+ reviews indicates consistently positive seller experiences
10+ retail locations across Arizona and California provide broad regional accessibility
Specialist staff includes coin numismatists and luxury watch experts in addition to gemologists
Private buying offices where items never leave the seller's sight during evaluation
cons: Transactions are final once an offer is accepted — no returns, cancellations, or second chances
The '40% more than competitors' claim is unverified marketing with no independent third-party confirmation
BBB complaint records include at least one customer dispute involving alleged legal threats over a negative Google review
No online price estimates or remote valuations — in-person visit required for any offer
BBB accreditation status is conflicting across Arizona and California state listings, making it difficult to verify independently
best_for: Individuals selling inherited or estate jewelry who want evaluation by certified gemologists
Coin collectors liquidating PCGS- or NGC-graded portfolios with numismatist expertise on-site
Sellers of luxury watches (Rolex, Cartier, Patek Philippe) seeking specialist buyers
Estate executors needing to appraise and sell multiple categories of valuables in a single visit
diagnosis: PMR is best suited for sellers who want a professional, multi-specialist in-person evaluation of high-value items — estate jewelry, graded coins, and luxury watches — from credentialed experts rather than generalist pawn shops. The main caveat is that offers are final once accepted and the '40% more' pricing claim is unverified, so sellers with significant items should obtain a competing offer before committing.
services: Silver buying (jewelry, flatware, coins, and bullion)
Luxury watch buying (Rolex, Cartier, Patek Philippe, and others)
